riskFactors: RBN is a legacy governance/protocol token, not a direct RWA claim instrument.
RGP-33 proposed migration from RBN to AEVO at 1:1 and winding down Ribbonomics, creating continuity and relevance risk for standalone RBN.
Current market snapshots show extremely low RBN trading volume and limited tracked markets.
No RBN-specific prospectus, legal opinion, fund administrator, transfer agent, or regulated-claim documentation was verified.
Etherscan shows no contract security audit submitted on the RBN token page, though Ribbon protocol docs reference audits for product contracts.
mitigants: Official Ribbon docs and governance forum provide clear protocol/tokenomics and migration context.
RBN token contract is publicly visible on Ethereum and source code is shown as verified/similar-match on Etherscan.
Ribbon protocol docs state product contracts were audited and maintain a bug bounty for specified contracts.
RBN holders were proposed to have a 1:1 path to AEVO governance, reducing some migration uncertainty if the route remains available.
assessmentMethod: Manual classification-first governance_protocol assessment using official docs, official governance proposal, Etherscan, and CoinGecko market snapshot. Scores are conservative because RBN is a legacy migration token with weak liquidity and no direct RWA claim.
